plant_profile.history: Unique History: Classified under the 'Dashamoola' (ten roots) and 'Angamarda-prashamana' (pain-relieving) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. The name 'Bruhati' signifies its 'vast' or 'large' stature as a medicinal agent for vital systems. Physical Characteristics: A perennial, prickly shrub growing up to 1-2 meters in height, with a spreading crown and star-shaped (stellate) hairs. It features prickly ovate-oblong leaves and attractive bluish-purple flowers arranged in cymes. The fruit is a globose, yellow drupe with a smooth surface. It is found in deciduous forests and wasteland throughout India and Southeast Asia. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Steroidal Alkaloids (Solasodine, Solasonine), Flavonoids, and Saponins. The presence of solasodine is crucial for its anti-inflammatory and cardio-protective action on the immune system. Strict Standardizations: The root is standardized in API based on macroscopic features (tough, woody bark, prickly appearance). Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<6%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>4%), and water-soluble extractive (>7%). HPTLC quantification is used to monitor solasodine content.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_modulation: Primarily pacifies Vata and Kapha doshas. Due to its Tikta (bitter) and Katu (pungent) Rasa and Ushna (heating) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Kasahara' (cough-relieving) and 'Shwasahara' (asthma-relieving) therapies, clearing the heavy and clogging qualities of Kapha. It may increase Pitta if used in excess.

medicinal_uses.respiratory_health_specialist: Bruhati is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for managing chronic cough, asthma, and bronchitis. Its Tikshna (sharp) and Ushna (heating) nature helps in liquefying and expelling thick, tenacious mucus from the lungs. WHO-aligned research supports its bronchodilatory and anti-asthmatic properties.
medicinal_uses.heart_and_cardiac_care: Acts as a mild Hridya (heart-tonic). It helps in strengthening the heart muscles and improving circulation by clearing Kapha-related obstructions in the blood vessels. It is traditionally used in 'Hrid-roga' (heart-diseases) treatments.
medicinal_uses.digestive_and_metabolic_care: Acts as a powerful 'Deepana' (appetizer) and 'Pachana' (digestive). It stimulates the 'Agni' (digestive fire) and helps in clearing 'Ama' (toxins) from the gut, reducing bloating and improving appetite.
medicinal_uses.fever_and_inflammation: Its Jwarahara (antipyretic) and Shothahara (anti-inflammatory) properties make it incomparable for managing chronic and inflammatory fevers. It helps in reducing systemic heat and localized swellings through its pungent-bitter action.
medicinal_uses.urinary_support: As a mild diuretic (Mutra-janana), it helps in relieving urinary tract infections and promoting healthy urine flow by clearing Vata-originated obstructions in the bladder.
medicinal_uses.hair_and_scalp_care: Its antimicrobial and blood-purifying properties help in treating chronic dandruff and scalp infections. It is traditionally used in 'Bala-roga' (childhood-ailments) to improve scalp health.
medicinal_uses.phytochemical_profile: Rich in Solasodine (a key steroidal alkaloid), Solamargine, Solasonine (glycoalkaloids), and various sterols that provide its multifaceted medicinal benefits, particularly its potent anti-inflammatory and cardio-tonic properties.

preparation_methods.root_decoction__kwatha_: 20g of root bark boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic respiratory congestion and fevers.
preparation_methods.root_powder__churna_: 3-6g of fine powder taken twice daily with honey (for cough) or warm water (for general digestion).
preparation_methods.medicated_oil__taila_: The whole plant decoction is cooked in sesame oil; used for traditional Ayurvedic massage in joint pain and localized swelling.
preparation_methods.medicated_ghee: The root is processed with cow's ghee to treat chronic heart diseases through its 'Hridya' (heart-tonic) quality.
preparation_methods.external_paste: Ground fresh leaves or bark applied directly to localized inflammations and boils to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.adults: Powder: 3-6 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Medicated Ghee: 5-10 g, divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.children: Generally safe. 1-2 ml of decoction or 125-250 mg of powder mixed with honey twice daily for respiratory protection during childhood infections.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related respiratory support and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 3g of powder daily with warm water at bedtime.

contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with severe Pitta (hyperacidity) should use it in moderation as it is Ushna (heating). Avoid during pregnancy due to its steroidal alkaloid content.
side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin warmth or nausea.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-asthmatic or cardiac medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in WHO monographs.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb with a history of use spanning millennia across various traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and TCM) without documented toxicity.
